title: 10 best herbal tinctures for bronchitis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its powerful anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The herb, also known as Echinacea, contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, its antiviral properties help to combat the underlying viral infection that often causes bronchitis. As a result, purple coneflower tinctures can provide relief from symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and chest tightness, allowing for faster recovery and reduced severity of the condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can alleviate congestion and ease breathing difficulties associated with bronchitis.

Antiviral: The tincture's antiviral properties help combat the viral infections that often cause bronchitis, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and chest tightness.
Mucolytic: Purple coneflower's mucolytic properties help break down and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel and alleviating coughing fits, congestion, and shortness of breath.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis.
Alkylamides: Alkylamides have immunomodulatory effects, which can help boost the immune system and fight off infections that cause bronchitis.
Cichoric acid: This polyphenolic compound has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat bacterial and viral infections that cause bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects.
Leaves: They are used due to their content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: They are used due to their rich content of saponins and alkylamides, which have immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in the fall when the plant is dormant.
Chop the roots and flowers into small pieces to release their active compounds.
Combine the chopped plant material with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1:5 by weight and transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and steep it in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and store the tincture in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The saponins present in licorice root help to reduce inflammation in the lungs, making it easier for air to pass through and relieving congestion. Additionally, licorice's expectorant properties help to loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up and clear out. This natural remedy can provide quick relief from bronchitis symptoms, helping to calm a sore throat, ease a persistent cough, and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis such as coughing and congestion.
Mucolytic properties: Licorice root's mucolytic properties help break down mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving chest congestion and coughing associated with bronchitis.
Antioxidant activity: Herbal licorice tinctures contain antioxidants that help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with bronchitis by reducing inflammation in the airways and lungs, thereby alleviating congestion and cough.
Licoricidin: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the mucous membranes in the bronchial tubes, reducing irritation and pain associated with bronchitis.
Liquiritigenin: It has expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and cough up excess mucus.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to soothe and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it effective in treating bronchitis.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to alleviate bronchitis symptoms.
Barks: They contain saponins and other compounds that have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and relieve bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra and clean them thoroughly with a soft brush.
Combine the cleaned roots with 500 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then filter it using a coffee filter.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with bronchitis because its active compound, mucilage, forms a soothing film in the throat and lungs, relieving inflammation and irritation. This thick, gel-like substance also helps to protect and calm the respiratory tract, reducing coughing and congestion. Additionally, marshmallow's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling in the airways, allowing for easier breathing and promoting the healing process. As a result, herbal marshmallow tinctures can provide quick relief from bronchitis symptoms, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating discomfort and promoting recovery.
remedy_properties: Mucilage production: Herbal marshmallow tinctures contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with bronchitis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving symptoms of bronchitis such as coughing and wheezing.

Expectorant properties: The mucilage in marshmallow tinctures also helps thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel from the lungs and sinuses, which can help alleviate congestion and chest tightness associated with bronchitis.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: These complex carbohydrates help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with bronchitis.

Iridoid glycosides (specifically, althaea root glycosides): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the inflammation and irritation of the airways, making it easier to breathe.

Phenolic acids (specifically, ferulic acid and caffeic acid): These antioxidants have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the severity of bronchitis by combating bacterial and viral infections, and minimizing tissue damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, which help soothe and protect inflamed tissues in the respiratory tract.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, beneficial for bronchitis treatment.
Buds: High in saponins and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of fresh roots of althaea officinalis during the spring or fall season.
Dry the collected roots at 40-50 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Steep 10 grams of the powdered root in 100 milliliters of 70 percent ethanol for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the solution through a cheesecloth and transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated respiratory tract tissues. The herb's mucilage content forms a protective barrier that coats the lungs and airways, reducing inflammation and congestion caused by bronchitis. Mullein tincture also contains antioxidants that help eliminate free radicals, which can exacerbate bronchial constriction and inflammation. By using mullein tinctures, individuals with bronchitis may experience relief from symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Mullein's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating congestion.
Mucolytic properties: The mucolytic agents in mullein tincture break down and thin out mucus, allowing for easier expulsion from the lungs and reducing coughing fits.
Expectorant properties: Mullein's expectorant qualities help to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and relieving bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, alleviating bronchitis symptoms.
Terpenoids (specifically Saponins): Terpenoids have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the inflammation in the airways associated with bronchitis, promoting easier breathing.

Phenolic Acids (specifically Apigenin and Luteolin): These flavonoids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect the respiratory tract from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures for bronchitis due to their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways.

Roots: The roots are used to make tinctures for bronchitis due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and combat infections in the respiratory tract.
Flowers: The flowers are used to make tinctures for bronchitis due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm coughs and reduce inflammation in the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried verbascum thapsus flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop the harvested verbascum thapsus flowers into smaller pieces to increase their surface area.
Combine the chopped verbascum thapsus flowers with 500 milliliters of 90% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking it occasionally, to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids after 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild potato tinctures helps with bronchitis because they possess potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm inflamed airways. The herbal remedy contains compounds like solanine, which has been shown to relax bronchial smooth muscle and reduce coughing spasms. Additionally, the tincture's expectorant properties help loosen mucus, making it easier to expel from the lungs, thereby relieving congestion and shortness of breath associated with bronchitis. As a natural remedy, wild potato tinctures provide a safe and effective way to alleviate bronchitis symptoms without pharmaceutical intervention.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Tomatoes and potatoes contain antioxidants like vitamin C and polyphenols, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs, alleviating symptoms of bronchitis.

Mucilage production: The mucilages present in tomatoes and potatoes may help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with bronchitis.

Expectorant properties: Some species within the Solanum family, like Solanum xanthocarpum, have been traditionally used as expectorants to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, which can help relieve congestion and coughs associated with bronchitis.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds may help alleviate bronchitis symptoms by their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation in the airways and relieving congestion.
Steroidal saponins: These saponins may help to combat bronchitis by exhibiting bronchodilatory effects, relaxing the airway muscles and increasing airflow, making it easier to breathe.

Triterpenoid saponins: These saponins may help to fight bronchitis by possessing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, combat infections, and promote healing in the respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their purported anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Leaves: Utilized for their supposed antioxidant and bronchodilatory effects.
Stems: Employed for their potential anti-inflammatory and mucolytic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the roots of euphorbia esculenta in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1:2 by weight.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated airways. The tincture's expectorant qualities also help loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, dandelion's natural antiviral and antibacterial properties help combat the underlying infection causing bronchitis, reducing congestion and coughing. By supporting lung health and promoting effective expulsion of mucus, dandelion tinctures provide a natural and holistic approach to managing bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dandelion tincture's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can alleviate bronchitis symptoms like coughing and wheezing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion tincture, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, can help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing bronchial irritation.

Mucilaginous properties: Dandelion tincture's mucilaginous compounds, like pectin, can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the lungs, reducing inflammation and coughing, while also supporting the body's natural mucus-clearing processes.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpene helps reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier for patients with bronchitis to breathe.
Inulin: A type of fructan, inulin has anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, alleviating bronchitis symptoms.

Phenolic acids (specifically, caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid): These compounds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat the oxidative stress and inflammation associated with bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract.
Roots: They are a rich source of inulin, a prebiotic fiber that supports gut health and immune function, which can aid in bronchitis recovery.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation, alleviate congestion, and promote a healthy respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the spring when the plant is in full growth.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Combine 1 part of dried taraxacum officinale material with 2 parts of 80% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place and shake the container every 2-3 days for 4-6 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herbal extract calms irritated airways, reducing inflammation and constriction, which alleviates coughing and congestion. Its antimicrobial compounds also help combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate bronchitis. By reducing swelling and suppressing the growth of harmful microorganisms, aloe tinctures provide natural relief from bronchial discomfort, making it an effective adjunct to traditional treatments for managing bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Mucolytic properties: The mucilages present in aloe vera tincture break down thick mucus and phlegm, allowing for easier expulsion from the lungs and relieving congestion.

Antimicrobial properties: Aloe vera's antimicrobial compounds, such as salicylic acid, help combat bacterial and viral infections that can exacerbate bronchitis symptoms, reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These glycosidic compounds help alleviate bronchitis symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation of bronchial muscles.
Aloin: A phenolic compound, aloin exhibits anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to clear mucus from the airways and ease bronchitis congestion.

Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates possess immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing bronchitis symptoms by regulating the immune response and soothing irritated airways.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help in reducing inflammation and calming the respiratory tract.
Buds: Rich in bioactive compounds that help in fighting off infections and reducing inflammation in the bronchial tubes.
Stems: Contain various glycosides and polysaccharides that have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, helping to alleviate bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the aloe vera leaves when they are mature and the gel inside is clear and abundant for tincture production.
Chop the aloe vera leaves into small pieces and combine them with a carrier oil like jojoba oil in a 1:2 ratio by weight.
Combine the aloe vera-oil mixture in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Place the jar in a cool dark place and steep the aloe vera in the oil for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the aloe vera-oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids before transferring the tincture to a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with bronchitis because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. The tincture's active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, also have expectorant properties, which loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, allowing for easier clearance. Additionally, ginger's natural antibacterial and antiviral properties help to combat infections that may be exacerbating bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, alleviating coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath.
Expectorant properties: The saponins present in ginger tincture help loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and reducing congestion and chest tightness.

Antioxidant and antiviral activity: Ginger's antioxidant and antiviral properties may help combat bronchitis-causing pathogens, such as viruses and bacteria, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting recovery.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpene compounds possess anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and promote the clearance of mucus in the airways, making it easier to breathe.

Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate bronchitis symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs.
Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has been shown to exhibit bronchodilatory effects, meaning it can help relax and dilate the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most used part due to their high concentration of active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Roots: Roots are also widely used as they contain similar active compounds as rhyzomes, which help alleviate bronchitis symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting mucus discharge.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass container.
Combine the rhizomes and vodka in the container and seal it to ensure they remain submerged.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks or 14-28 days to allow for extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh sieve into another container to separate the solids.
Store the resulting tincture in a cool dark place and use within 2 years after the date of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jesuit's bark tinctures helps with bronchitis because it contains a high concentration of catechin, a powerful antioxidant that soothes inflamed airways and reduces congestion. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to calm coughing spasms, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, its expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, allowing for better oxygenation and reduced respiratory distress. By addressing the underlying inflammation and congestion, Jesuit's bark tinctures provides natural relief from bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Cinchona Alkaloids: These alkaloids, particularly quinine and quinidine, have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which help to relieve bronchial constriction and reduce inflammation in the airways.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids present in Jesuit's Bark have been shown to exhibit expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and breathe more easily.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, reducing bronchial spasms and improving breathing.
remedy_constituents: Quinine Alkaloid: Quinine has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and congestion in the bronchial passages, making it easier to breathe.
Quinidine Alkaloid: Quinidine has a bronchodilatory effect, which means it helps to relax the muscles in the airways, improving airflow and reducing wheezing associated with bronchitis.
Quinovine Alkaloid: Quinovine has a mucolytic effect, helping to break down mucus and make it easier to expel, which can help alleviate coughing and congestion symptoms associated with bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cinchona officinalis are used to make tinctures for bronchitis because they contain a high concentration of quinine, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms of bronchitis.

Barks: The barks of Cinchona officinalis are used to make tinctures for bronchitis because they are rich in alkaloids, including quinine and quinidine, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways.

Stems: The stems of Cinchona officinalis are used to make tinctures for bronchitis because they contain a combination of alkaloids, including quinine and quinidine, which help reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and promote expectoration, making it easier to cough up mucus and other debris from the lungs.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of cinchona officinalis bark and 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka for tincture production.
Cut the cinchona bark into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction and infuse.
Combine the cinchona pieces with the vodka in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks to allow for infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butterbur tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the airways and reduce swelling in the lungs. The herbal extract also possesses expectorant qualities, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the chest, allowing for easier breathing and relief from congestion. Additionally, butterbur's ability to calm spasms in the bronchial tubes eases coughing fits and makes it easier to breathe, providing welcome relief to those suffering from bronchitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Butterbur tincture contains compounds like petasins, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, alleviating bronchitis symptoms.

Antispasmotic: The flavonoids present in butterbur tincture help relax smooth muscle spasms in the airways, relieving bronchospasms and making it easier to breathe during an acute episode of bronchitis.
Mucolytic: Butterbur's mucolytic properties help break down excess mucus and facilitate its clearance from the lungs, reducing congestion and coughing associated with bronchitis.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating bronchitis symptoms.

Petasins: These sesquiterpenes have potent anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, which help relax the airway muscles and improve lung function, making it easier to breathe and relieve bronchitis symptoms.
Petasolide: This iridoid glycoside has antioxidant properties that help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to bronchitis development and progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the most used part for making tinctures due to their high concentration of petasin and isopetasin, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used to make tinctures because they contain a higher amount of sesquiterpenes, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects.
Rhyzomes : Rhyzomes are used to make tinctures due to their higher concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help in reducing inflammation and promoting respiratory health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of fresh petasites hybridus roots or use 100-200 grams of dried roots in late summer or early fall.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped roots with 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Japanese honeysuckle tinctures helps with bronchitis because it contains compounds that effectively soothe and calm inflamed airways, reducing coughing and congestion. The tincture's antiviral properties also help to combat the underlying infection causing bronchitis, promoting a faster recovery. Additionally, its expectorant qualities aid in clearing mucus from the lungs, making breathing easier and more comfortable. remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Japanese Honeysuckle contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, including those that cause bronchitis, helping to reduce the severity and duration of the infection.

Expectorant properties: The tincture's saponins and flavonoids help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and reducing congestion, which is a common symptom of bronchitis.

Rhyzomes: Contains pungent and bitter principles that stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory symptoms such as coughing and wheezing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lonicera japonica flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has dried off.
Cleanly chop 4 ounces of freshly harvested material into small pieces to ensure proper extraction.
Combine chopped plant material with 100 proof vodka in a clean glass jar, filling it to about an inch from the top.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate solvent extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding solids and transferring liquid tincture to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass tinctures helps with bronchitis because it possesses potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that effectively combat bacterial and viral infections, reducing inflammation in the airways. The herb's citral content also relaxes respiratory muscles, easing coughing and congestion. Additionally, lemongrass has natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways. Antimicrobial property: Lemongrass tincture exhibits antimicrobial activity against bacteria and viruses that can cause bronchitis, helping to combat infections and promote healing.

Rhyzomes: Lemongrass rhyzomes contain oils that help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tinctures for bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cymbopogon citratus leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Cleanse and dry the harvested leaves to remove impurities and excess moisture using a clean cloth.
Chop the dried leaves into small pieces to increase their surface area for better extraction.
Steep 1 part of chopped cymbopogon citratus in 2 parts of high-proof vodka or ethanol at room temperature for 6 hours.
Strain and filter the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into dark glass bottles and store for 6 weeks before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its natural antispasmodic and expectorant properties. The herbal extract soothes inflamed airways, relaxing constricted bronchial muscles to ease wheezing and shortness of breath. Rosemary's ability to thin mucus also helps to expel it from the lungs, reducing congestion and coughing. Expectorant properties: Rosemary's expectorant compounds help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion associated with bronchitis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can alleviate symptoms of bronchitis such as chest tightness and difficulty breathing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and congestion associated with bronchitis.
Barks: The barks of the rosemary plant contain compounds that help to relax the respiratory tract muscles, which can alleviate breathing difficulties and coughing symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers.
Combine one part of the harvested plant material with four parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks allowing the herbal extract to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Label and store the resulting tincture in a cool dark place for up to two years taking 20-30 drops twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the airways and relieve coughing spasms. The Boswellia resin present in frankincense tinctures has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, thereby reducing swelling and congestion in the lungs and bronchial tubes. Additionally, frankincense's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe and aiding in the healing process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Frankincense tinctures contain boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, helping to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis such as coughing and congestion.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds present in frankincense tinctures help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate bronchitis symptoms and slow down healing.
Expectorant properties: Frankincense tinctures have a natural expectorant effect, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways and lungs, making it easier to breathe and expel phlegm.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the frankincense plant contain a high concentration of resin, which has anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe irritated airways.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of frankincense have been traditionally used in traditional medicine to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality boswellia serrata roots and wash them thoroughly under running water.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and soak them in a mixture of ethanol and glycerin for 14 days.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids after 7 days.
Store the tincture in dark glass bottles at room temperature for 6 months to allow it to mature.
Filter the tincture through a coffee filter before using it to treat bronchitis symptoms such as coughing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. The curcuminoids present in turmeric inhibit the production of prostaglandins, known to cause constriction of airways and increase mucus production. Additionally, turmeric's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and viral infections that can exacerbate bronchitis symptoms. By reducing swelling and fighting off underlying infections, herbal turmeric tinctures provide relief from coughing, wheezing, and chest congestion associated with bronchitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's main active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and congestion in the lungs and airways associated with bronchitis.

Antimicrobial properties: Turmeric has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various bacterial and viral pathogens that can cause bronchitis, such as Streptococcus pneumoniae and influenza virus, helping to combat infection and promote healing.

Expectorant properties: The bioactive compounds in turmeric tincture, particularly curcuminoids, have been found to stimulate the production of mucus and thin its consistency, making it easier to cough up and clear from the lungs, thereby relieving bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, which are the underground stems of the turmeric plant, contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe respiratory inflammation associated with bronchitis.

Roots: Turmeric roots are rich in bioactive compounds like volatile oils and polyphenols, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis by reducing cough and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh curcuma longa rhizomes in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested rhizomes in a warm place or using a food dehydrator at low heat.
Grind 200g of dried rhizomes into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground rhizome powder with 1l of 80% ethanol vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring full immersion for 14 days.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and transfer to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with bronchitis because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the airways, reducing inflammation and congestion. The tincture's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, fennel's antispasmodic properties relax the muscles in the respiratory tract, reducing coughing fits and wheezing. By addressing these underlying symptoms, herbal fennel tinctures can provide relief from bronchitis-related discomfort and help speed up recovery.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Fennel's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion in the chest.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, which can help to ease coughing and difficulty breathing associated with bronchitis.
Antispasmodic properties: Fennel's antispasmodic properties relax and calm the smooth muscle lining the airways, reducing spasms that can cause wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in fiber and antioxidants, fennel roots help soothe coughs and reduce inflammation associated with bronchitis.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a volatile oil that has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried foeniculum vulgare leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated and store them in an airtight container.
Combine one part dried foeniculum vulgare leaves with one part high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar to create the tincture mixture.
Allow the mixture to steep for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily to ensure proper infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the foeniculum vulgare tincture.
Transfer the foeniculum vulgare tincture to glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in the refrigerator at 2-3 degrees celsius for up to two years.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for bronchitis are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants that help alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, a respiratory condition characterized by inflammation and congestion in the lungs. These tinctures are effective in soothing coughs, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation, making them a popular natural remedy for bronchitis. The best herbal tinctures for bronchitis include Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Thymus vulgaris, Sambucus nigra, Verbascum thapsus, Hydrastis canadensis, Lavandula angustifolia, Zingiber officinale, and Mentha x piperita. If left untreated, bronchitis can lead to chronic respiratory problems, fatigue, and decreased quality of life, making it essential to incorporate these herbal remedies into your treatment plan.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bronchitis that are treated with herbal tinctures are chronic coughs, asthma, and COPD. Herbs such as thyme, eucalyptus, and peppermint have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce congestion and ease breathing. Tinctures of these herbs can be used in combination with other natural remedies to alleviate symptoms and promote relaxation. Additionally, tinctures of ginger and turmeric may also be beneficial for reducing inflammation and relieving respiratory discomfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with bronchitis is a blend of thyme, eucalyptus, and ginger. Thyme's antimicrobial properties help combat infections, while eucalyptus' expectorant properties aid in loosening and clearing mucus from the lungs. Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties reduce congestion and soothe the respiratory tract. This trio can be taken in a 1:1:1 ratio, 2-3 times a day, mixed with water or taken sublingually. Consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have underlying health conditions or take medications.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by The Korean journal of internal medicine, herbal tinctures like HL301, which is a combined product of seven medicinal plants, have shown to be effective in treating acute bronchitis. The study involved 166 patients with acute bronchitis, who were randomly assigned to receive either HL301 (600 mg/day) or a placebo for 7 days. The results showed that those treated with HL301 experienced a significant reduction in bronchitis severity score, improved symptoms, and higher response, improvement, and satisfaction rates compared to the placebo group.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its decongestant and expectorant properties. The active compound, eucalyptol, has a natural ability to open airways and relieve congestion, making it easier to breathe. It also helps to thin mucus, allowing it to be coughed up more easily, providing temporary relief from bronchitis symptoms. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and promote healing, providing natural relief from bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps with bronchitis by acting as an expectorant, loosening and removing mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up.
Beta-caryophyllene: Relieves bronchitis symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response to alleviate congestion and discomfort.
Globulol: Assists in bronchitis relief by displaying antimicrobial properties, reducing the growth of bacteria that can exacerbate respiratory infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part for making tinctures due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a primary active ingredient that helps relieve bronchitis symptoms.
Buds: The buds are also used to make tinctures, as they contain a higher concentration of eucalyptol than the leaves and other parts, making them highly effective in treating bronchitis.
Barks: The barks are another commonly used part for making tinctures, as they are rich in eucalyptol and other compounds that help alleviate bronchitis symptoms and promote respiratory health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and flowers for every 2 parts of vodka by volume.
Clean and prepare the harvested eucalyptus globulus leaves and flowers for tincture preparation.
Combine 100g of vodka with 50g of prepared eucalyptus globulus leaves and flowers in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking required.
Strain the liquid from the jar through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. It stimulates the production of white blood cells, enhancing the body's natural defense against infections. The tincture's antioxidants and flavonoids help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the respiratory tract, relieving congestion and coughing. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus, facilitating easier breathing and reducing the severity of bronchitis symptoms. This natural remedy supports the body's recovery process and promotes overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help with bronchitis by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune system, which can alleviate symptoms such as coughing and congestion.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Echinacea purpurea have antioxidant properties that help protect the lungs from oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development and progression of bronchitis.

Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides in Echinacea purpurea have immunomodulatory effects, which can enhance the body's natural defenses against respiratory infections, such as bronchitis, by stimulating the production of white blood cells.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as alkylamides and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help alleviate bronchitis symptoms.
Flowers: They contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in late summer or early fall after the first frost.
Dry the harvested echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1 part dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers with 2 parts 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every day or two.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The tincture is derived from the root of the licorice plant, which contains compounds like glycyrrhizin that have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the airways. This helps to alleviate congestion, coughing, and difficulty breathing associated with bronchitis. Additionally, the tincture's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, promoting a more effective recovery from bronchitis and relieving symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help with bronchitis by reducing inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Terpenoids: They help with bronchitis by exhibiting expectorant properties, allowing mucus to be more easily coughed up, and by reducing inflammation in the lungs.
Glycyrrhizin: This compound helps with bronchitis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, and by exhibiting anti-microbial properties to prevent infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Leaves: The leaves are another commonly used part, as they contain flavonoids and saponins that help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract.
Barks: The barks are also used in tincture form, as they contain glycosides and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote expectoration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh or 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots for tincture production in early morning.
Clean and chop the harvested roots into small pieces to ensure uniform extraction.
Combine 50g of chopped glycyrrhiza glabra roots with 50% vodka as solvent in a 1:2 ratio.
Store the mixture in a clean glass container and macerate for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its expectorant properties, which aid in relieving congestion and coughs associated with the condition. The thymol content in the tincture has antibacterial properties, helping to combat underlying infections that exacerbate bronchitis. Additionally, thymus vulgaris tinctures contain mucolytic agents that break down mucus, making it easier to expel, and anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the airways, providing relief from bronchitis symptoms. This natural remedy can be a valuable tool in managing bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Carvacrol: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, reducing the severity of bronchitis by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that can exacerbate the condition.
Rosmarinic acid: Possesses anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation in the airways and helping to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, such as coughing and congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in thymol, a compound with antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate bronchitis symptoms.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain thymol and other volatile oils that have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract.
Barks: Thyme bark is another source of thymol and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for soothing bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in late summer when they are in full bloom for optimal potency and flavor.
Gently chop 100 grams of the freshly harvested leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter.
Combine the chopped leaves with 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Place the jar in a cool dark place and steep it for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the mixture every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tincture's high concentration of isorhapontigenin and other bioactive compounds has been shown to help alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, such as congestion and coughing. By reducing inflammation in the airways, Sambucus nigra tincture helps to open up the bronchial tubes, allowing for easier breathing and relieving discomfort. This natural remedy can provide fast relief and support the body's ability to recover from bronchitis.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and combat oxidative stress in the respiratory tract, thereby alleviating bronchitis symptoms.
Phenolic acids: These compounds exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help fight off infections causing bronchitis, and also have anti-inflammatory properties to soothe the respiratory tract.

Sambunigrin: It is a glycoside that has been shown to possess antiviral properties, which can help combat viral infections, such as those causing bronchitis, by inhibiting the replication of the virus.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Sambucus nigra are used due to their high content of antiviral compounds, such as sambunigrin, which help to reduce inflammation and combat bronchitis.
Leaves: The leaves of Sambucus nigra are used for their expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the bronchial tubes, relieving symptoms of bronchitis.
Stems: The stems of Sambucus nigra are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and irritation in the bronchial tubes, providing relief from bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in late spring or early summer when they are in full bloom.
Clean and dry the plant material by spreading it out in a single layer on paper towels for 2-3 days.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife or a herb cutter to release oils.
Steep the chopped plant material in a 60-80 proof spirit such as vodka or glycerin in a ratio of 1:5 for 2-6 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its expectorant properties, which aid in loosening and clearing mucus from the lungs. The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help soothe irritated airways, reducing coughing and congestion. Additionally, Verbascum thapsus contains mucolytic agents that break down thick mucus, making it easier to expel. By reducing inflammation and loosening mucus, Verbascum thapsus tinctures can provide relief from bronchitis symptoms, promoting a smoother breathing experience and overall recovery.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing inflammation and soothing the respiratory tract, thereby alleviating bronchitis symptoms.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in Verbascum thapsus have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in protecting the lungs from oxidative stress and inflammation, and promoting recovery from bronchitis.

Saponins: Saponins in Verbascum thapsus have been found to possess expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used to make tinctures for bronchitis due to their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs.

Stems: Stems are used to make tinctures for bronchitis as they contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antitussive effects, reducing cough and inflammation in the respiratory system.
Flowers: Flowers are used to make tinctures for bronchitis because of their mucilaginous properties, which help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh or dried verbascum thapsus flowers at the peak of potency usually in mid to late summer.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm dark place for 2 to 3 weeks to reduce moisture.
Combine 1 part of dried flowers with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean dark glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks to allow the infusion to mature.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis tinctures helps with bronchitis because it contains berberine, an alkaloid with potent antimicrobial properties that targets the root cause of the infection, reducing inflammation and congestion in the lungs. It also has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, Hydrastis canadensis tinctures has immunomodulatory effects, supporting the body's natural defense mechanisms and promoting overall respiratory health, providing relief from bronchitis symptoms and supporting the healing process.
remedy_constituents: Isoquinoline Alkaloids: These compounds help with bronchitis by reducing inflammation and exhibiting expectorant properties, making it easier to cough up mucus.
Tannins: Tannins in Hydrastis canadensis have astringent properties that help reduce inflammation and alleviate coughing associated with bronchitis.
Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids in the herb possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat underlying infections and reduce the severity of bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of berberine, a key compound responsible for the plant's medicinal properties and effectiveness in treating bronchitis.
Leaves: They contain berberine and other alkaloids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, beneficial in reducing bronchitis symptoms.
Rhyzomes: They are a rich source of berberine and other compounds that exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them valuable for creating tinctures that target bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hydrastis canadensis roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full maturity stage.
Clean and dry the harvested roots to prevent contamination and preserve their potency for tincture making.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or a food processor to increase their surface area.
Combine the chopped roots with 80 to 100 proof vodka in a 1 to 5 ratio by weight in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking to extract the medicinal compounds.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which soothe the airways and reduce inflammation. The tincture's antimicrobial action helps combat infections that can cause bronchitis, such as bacterial or viral infections. Additionally, the calming effects of lavender can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate bronchitis symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, Lavandula angustifolia tinctures can provide relief from bronchitis symptoms and support the body's natural healing process.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps reduce inflammation in the airways, relieving symptoms of bronchitis.
Linalyl acetate: Exerts anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, easing bronchial spasms and promoting relaxation.
Caryophyllene oxide: Demonstrates antimicrobial properties, reducing the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their antiseptic and expectorant properties, which help relieve congestion and cough associated with bronchitis.
Flowers: They are used for their calming effects, which help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, alleviating symptoms of bronchitis.
Stems: They are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and 750 milliliters of vodka for tincture preparation.
Combine the flowers with vodka in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly to prevent contamination.
Place the jar in a cool dark place and steep for 2 weeks to allow for extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container to remove solids.
Transfer the tincture to smaller dropper bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to ease congestion and reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. The tincture's ability to thin mucus and promote its expulsion can help alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, such as coughing and wheezing. By soothing the respiratory tract, ginger tincture can provide relief and support the body's natural healing process.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: They help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe for individuals suffering from bronchitis.
Shogaols: These compounds help to relax the airway muscles, thereby opening up the airways and relieving bronchitis symptoms.
Gingerols' and Shogaols' related compound Gingerdiol: It exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Aromatic and spicy part that contains the essential oils responsible for its medicinal properties.
Roots: The primary part used for medicinal purposes, as it stores the highest concentration of active compounds.
Leaves: Used in some herbal remedies due to their volatile oils, although less commonly used than roots or rhyzomes for bronchitis treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of fresh zingiber officinale roots in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Chop the roots into small pieces using a sharp knife to release their active compounds quickly.
Combine 2 cups of the chopped roots with 4 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tinctures helps with bronchitis because it contains menthol, a natural expectorant that thins and loosens mucus, making it easier to cough up. The tincture's antibacterial properties also help reduce inflammation and combat infection in the lungs. Additionally, menthol's decongestant properties help open up airways, allowing for better airflow and reducing congestion. As a result, the tincture can provide relief from bronchitis symptoms, such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath, helping to alleviate discomfort and promote recovery.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps reduce inflammation and relax bronchial spasms, making it easier to breathe and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): As a decongestant and expectorant, cineole helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, reducing congestion and coughing associated with bronchitis.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, thereby alleviating symptoms of bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Mentha x piperita for making tinctures to treat bronchitis, due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have antimicrobial and expectorant properties.
Stems: The stems are often used in conjunction with the leaves to create a more potent tincture, as they contain similar compounds that help to relieve bronchitis symptoms.

Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita are sometimes used to make a tincture, as they contain menthol and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh leaves of mentha x piperita at the peak of their fragrance and flavor intensity.
Clean the harvested leaves thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 200g of the cleaned leaves with 500ml of 95% ethanol in a large glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow the tincture to mature.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and transfer it to dark glass bottles for storage.
